title: OT/ICS Threat Intel - Power
description: Curated OT/ICS cybersecurity advisories, incidents and threat intelligence from primary sources. Editorial summaries by Mike Holcomb in partnership with UtilSec.

_otthreatfeed.usage: Free to reuse with attribution. Credit 'Mike Holcomb & UtilSec' and link back to otthreatfeed.com and to each item's linked primary source. Summaries are editorial paraphrase - verify against the primary source before acting.

items.title: Russia’s FSB Center 16 is harvesting router configs across the energy sector — joint advisory names the grid
items.content_text: On 13 July, NSA, CISA, the FBI and partner agencies from a dozen countries issued joint advisory AA26-194A, attributing a long-running campaign against poorly configured routers to Russia’s FSB Center 16 (also tracked as Static Tundra, Berserk Bear and Dragonfly). The actors scan for routers exposing SNMP with default community strings and abuse Cisco Smart Install to copy device configurations off to attacker infrastructure — handing them network maps, credentials and VPN details. Energy is named among the most-targeted sectors, and CERT Polska has tied the same actor to the attempted December 2025 wiper attack on Poland’s grid. For power utilities the exposure is concrete: the edge routers linking substations, EMS and control centres are exactly the gear being harvested, and a stolen running-config is a blueprint of your OT network.

_otthreatfeed.source_name: CISA AA26-194A / NSA / FBI — corroborated by Nextgov, SecurityWeek
_otthreatfeed.date_note: Joint advisory published 13 Jul 2026; campaign ongoing

items.title: Poland’s grid was the target of an attempted wiper last winter — now tied to the same FSB unit
items.content_text: CERT Polska has attributed the attempted December 2025 DynoWiper attack on Poland’s power grid to Static Tundra/FSB Center 16, the actor behind this week’s router advisory (ESET and Dragos attribute the wiper itself to Sandworm with moderate confidence). The attack, which would have cut power to roughly half a million people in winter, was thwarted. It is foundational context for the top story: the same access tradecraft that harvests configs is what precedes a destructive attempt.

_otthreatfeed.source_name: CERT Polska / ESET / Dragos — via SecurityOnline
_otthreatfeed.date_note: Attempt Dec 2025; re-attributed in 13 Jul 2026 joint advisory

items.title: VOLTZITE keeps burrowing into US electric utilities — and it is studying what makes a plant trip
items.content_text: Dragos’ electric-grid analysis describes VOLTZITE (the ICS-capable arm of Volt Typhoon) receiving footholds from access broker SYLVANITE and using them to pull configuration files and alarm data from engineering workstations — specifically probing which conditions would trigger a process shutdown. This is not espionage for its own sake; it is target development for disruption. It is foundational context for the current nation-state picture rather than a fresh incident.

items.title: Dragos’ year-in-review: threat groups now map whole control loops, and ransomware rose 49%
items.content_text: Dragos’ 2026 Year in Review reports adversaries advancing from isolated-device targeting to mapping entire control loops — HMIs, variable-frequency drives, metering modules and cellular gateways — with KAMACITE running sustained reconnaissance of US industrial devices from March through July 2025. Industrial-ransomware victims rose 49% year-on-year. For power operators it is landscape context: the reconnaissance economy feeding grid-capable groups is maturing.

items.title: Rockwell 1715-AENTR EtherNet/IP adapter exposes an unauthenticated debug port — energy-flagged
items.content_text: CISA advisory ICSA-26-195-04 covers a Rockwell Automation 1715-AENTR EtherNet/IP adapter that exposes a network-accessible debug port without enforcing privilege controls, allowing unauthenticated remote access to intrusive CLI commands. CISA flags the affected sectors as Energy, Water and Wastewater, and Critical Manufacturing. On a substation or generation LAN, unauthenticated CLI to a redundancy adapter is a direct path to reading files, stopping tasks and altering I/O state.

items.title: July ICS Patch Tuesday: a CVSS 10 Siemens auth bypass and a critical SIMATIC S7-1500 fix
items.content_text: Siemens’ July advisories include six critical-rated issues, led by a CVSS 10 token-invalidation flaw in Opencenter X that lets an attacker bypass authentication for full application access, plus critical fixes in SIMATIC S7-1500, Mendix, Sidis and Desigo CC. Rockwell and Schneider shipped concurrent advisories. Utilities running SIMATIC controllers or Siemens energy-automation software should treat this cycle as a priority patch window.

items.title: Hitachi Energy EMS and planning tools flagged in CISA’s 7 July batch
items.content_text: CISA’s 7 July advisory batch included Hitachi Energy products used in the power sector — PROMOD V (market and planning) and the e-mesh energy-management system — alongside Siemens SINEC OS, Mendix and other items. These are the higher-value grid applications rather than field devices; vulnerabilities here reach market operations and DER/storage management rather than a single relay.

items.title: Schneider Easergy MiCOM Px40 relays expose device data via hard-coded SNMP credentials
items.content_text: CISA advisory ICSA-26-190-03 (9 July) covers CVE-2026-4832, a use-of-hard-coded-credentials flaw (CVSS 5.3) letting an attacker read sensitive information from Easergy MiCOM Px40 protection relays over SNMP. It is medium-severity and information-disclosure only, but on a substation LAN it feeds exactly the reconnaissance the FSB campaign relies on — and protection relays are core to grid safety.

items.title: CIP-012-2 is now in force; CIP-003-9 tightens the low-impact and vendor-access perimeter
items.content_text: NERC CIP-012-2 became enforceable on 1 July 2026, strengthening protection of real-time operational data exchanged between control centres. It follows CIP-003-9 (effective 1 April 2026), which expands governance for low-impact BES Cyber Systems with a focus on vendor electronic remote access and supply-chain risk. Both map directly onto this cycle’s threats: control-centre data protection and third-party remote-access control.

_otthreatfeed.source_name: NERC / Ampyx Cyber CIP roadmap analysis
_otthreatfeed.date_note: CIP-012-2 effective 1 Jul 2026; CIP-003-9 effective 1 Apr 2026
